# Breast Cancer Screening Law Leaves Some With Unexpected Bills

**TL;DR:** A Virginia law that eliminates breast cancer screening costs is not preventing all patients from receiving bills they shouldn't owe.

Virginia enacted a law designed to eliminate out-of-pocket costs for breast cancer screening, but some residents are still receiving unexpected bills for covered services. The gap appears to stem from providers and insurers not fully implementing or correctly billing under the law. Patients who receive questionable bills should verify their coverage and challenge charges they believe are incorrect.

**Why it matters:** Chesterfield residents seeking breast cancer screening should know their rights under this law and take steps to dispute any bills for covered screenings.

[Read the source at Google News: Virginia Government →](https://news.google.?oc=5)
